surface: Right chat rail — quick action chips and empty state
observed: Quick action chips show static prompts (@nodeagent diligence CardioNova, @nodeagent runway gaps) regardless of which artifact is active. Empty state text is generic: 'Ask the room agent to work on the seeded model.'
whyItMatters: When a user opens the Diligence memo, the chat rail still suggests 'diligence CardioNova' and 'runway gaps' instead of 'draft memo' or 'review memo'. A first-time user cannot tell what NodeAgent can do for the currently selected surface.
allowedChanges: compute contextual prompts based on activeArtifactId and artifact kind/title
update empty state hint text based on active artifact
fall back to default prompts when no artifact is active
proofRequired: verify chips change when switching artifacts
beforeScreenshot: Playwright MCP transient — DOM evidence: wall active, chips were [@nodeagent diligence CardioNova, @nodeagent runway gaps]
afterScreenshot: Playwright MCP transient — DOM evidence: wall active, chips became [@nodeagent organize wall, @nodeagent diligence CardioNova]; runway active, chips became [@nodeagent populate runway, @nodeagent find evidence gaps]; diligence memo active, chips became [@nodeagent draft memo, @nodeagent review memo]
whyNotRedesign: The chat panel layout is correct. The issue was purely that the quick-action chips and empty state text were hardcoded instead of responding to the active artifact context. A Level 0 copy/state fix (useMemo based on activeArtifactId) fully resolves the issue without touching layout.
